commands.repair_reason: Contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the session-history compatibility risk are still needed; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.; Cleared: The diff is limited to TypeScript heartbeat filtering, tests, and a LINE plugin type import; I found no concrete security or supply-chain concern. commands.review_summary:
The PR expands heartbeat transcript filtering in embedded agent prompt assembly, adds heartbeat response-tool regression coverage, and adjusts the LINE plugin command type import.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main only filters heartbeat prompt plus immediate OK pairs, while response-tool heartbeat calls/results flow through the embedded-runner history filtering call sites. I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the session-history compatibility risk are still needed; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: Filter heartbeat response-tool transcript artifacts
target.body:
## Summary
- Filters full silent heartbeat transcript spans before normal prompt assembly, including helper tool calls/results, prompt-only interrupted wakes, pre-terminal assistant status text, and `heartbeat_respond notify:false`.
- Preserves visible heartbeat output only when it is actually visible: free-text alerts remain, and `heartbeat_respond notify:true` is kept only after a completed successful matching response-tool result.
- Fixes bundled LINE extension lint by using the public command definition type instead of deriving it through a broad API parameter.

## Verification
Behavior addressed: Later normal prompts no longer receive stale silent heartbeat artifacts such as `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]`, `HEARTBEAT.md`, `heartbeat_respond`, `notify=false`, pre-terminal heartbeat status text, or pending `notify:true` calls without completed tool results; completed visible heartbeat alerts remain in prompt snapshots.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with temp session stores and embedded runner/context-engine harness; local `runHeartbeatOnce` heartbeat response-tool harness. No live provider or real Telegram send was used.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.browser-import.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:canary`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/line/index.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --uncommitted > /tmp/openclaw-heartbeat-codex-review-final2.log 2>&1`
Evidence after fix: Vitest reported `Test Files 7 passed (7)` and `Tests 154 passed (154)`. The context-engine tests prove silent heartbeat artifacts are absent from `messagesSnapshot`, while visible free-text alerts and completed `notify:true` heartbeat response-tool notifications are retained.
Observed result after fix: Normal prompt snapshots for `what model are you` do not contain `HEARTBEAT.md`, `heartbeat_respond`, `notify=false`, `"notify":false`, pending `"notify":true`, pre-terminal heartbeat status text, or `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]` after silent/interrupted heartbeats; visible alert text and completed provider-shaped `notify:true` responses keyed by `call_id`/`tool_use_id` stay present.
What was not tested: No live provider dispatch or real Telegram delivery; the proof uses local runner/session harnesses plus local static/type/lint checks.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
